name: Taoyuan (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the agricultural plains and forests of Taoyuan
warmth, vitality and the peach blossom that gives the city its name (桃園, 'Peach Garden')

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem in green and orange, depicting a peach blossom (the city's symbol) along with abstract letter forms, and the wordmark 'Taoyuan'.
history: Taoyuan was upgraded from a county to Taiwan's sixth special municipality on 25 December 2014. The city flag was adopted at the time of the upgrade. The peach-blossom motif derives directly from the city's name (桃園, literally 'Peach Garden'), which dates to the early Qing period when Han Chinese settlers planted extensive peach orchards in the area.

features.symbolism: stylised peach blossom, alluding directly to the city's name 桃園 ('Peach Garden') and the orchards planted by early Han Chinese settlers, expressing Taoyuan's agricultural heritage and modern dynamism
